# quantmsdiann — Full Reference

> Nextflow pipeline for DIA quantitative proteomics powered by DIA-NN.

## Overview

quantmsdiann processes DIA (Data-Independent Acquisition) mass spectrometry data using DIA-NN as the core engine. It supports two modes:

1. Library-free: Raw DIA files + FASTA → in-silico spectral library generation (DIA-NN predict) → DIA-NN analysis → protein quantification
2. Library-based: Raw DIA files + empirical spectral library → DIA-NN analysis → protein quantification

## Installation

### Prerequisites
- Nextflow >= 21.10.3
- Container runtime: Docker (recommended), Singularity, or Conda

### Run with Docker
nextflow run bigbio/quantmsdiann -r main -profile docker \
  --input samplesheet.csv \
  --database proteins.fasta \
  --outdir results/

### Run with Singularity (HPC)
nextflow run bigbio/quantmsdiann -r main -profile singularity \
  --input samplesheet.csv \
  --database proteins.fasta \
  --outdir results/

## Input Files

### Samplesheet (CSV)
spectra_file,sdrf_file
/path/to/sample1.raw,/path/to/sdrf.tsv
/path/to/sample2.raw,/path/to/sdrf.tsv

### SDRF File
Tab-separated file following the SDRF-Proteomics standard. Key columns:
- source name: sample identifier
- characteristics[organism]: species
- comment[instrument]: mass spectrometer
- comment[precursor mass tolerance]: search tolerance
- comment[fragment mass tolerance]: fragment tolerance

### FASTA Database
Standard UniProt FASTA. Used for in-silico library generation in library-free mode.

## Key Parameters

### DIA-NN Settings
- --diann_normalize true (default: normalize across runs)
- --diann_library_generation true (library-free mode)
- --diann_speclib /path/to/library.tsv (library-based mode)
- --mass_acc_ms1 10 (MS1 mass accuracy ppm)
- --mass_acc_ms2 20 (MS2 mass accuracy ppm)

### FDR
- --fdr_cutoff 0.01 (1% FDR at precursor and protein level)

### Performance
- --diann_threads 8 (threads per DIA-NN process)
- --max_memory 32.GB

## Output Files

results/
├── diann_report/
│   ├── report.tsv              # Main DIA-NN report
│   ├── report.pr_matrix.tsv    # Precursor-level matrix
│   ├── report.pg_matrix.tsv    # Protein group matrix
│   └── report.gg_matrix.tsv    # Gene group matrix
├── msstats/
│   └── msstats_input.csv       # MSstats-compatible CSV
├── pmultiqc/
│   └── multiqc_report.html     # Interactive QC report
└── pipeline_info/

## Common Workflows

### Library-free DIA analysis
nextflow run bigbio/quantmsdiann -r main -profile docker \
  --input samplesheet.csv \
  --database uniprot_human.fasta \
  --diann_library_generation true \
  --outdir results/

### Library-based DIA analysis
nextflow run bigbio/quantmsdiann -r main -profile docker \
  --input samplesheet.csv \
  --diann_speclib spectral_library.tsv \
  --outdir results/

## Integration with Ecosystem

- QC reports: pmultiqc generates reports from quantmsdiann output
- Downstream quantification: mokume can process DIA-NN protein matrices
- Data format: qpx converts output to QPX Parquet format
- Reanalyzed data: quantms Portal hosts DIA datasets processed with quantmsdiann

## Troubleshooting

- Out of memory during library generation: increase --max_memory
- Low protein identifications: check SDRF organism matches FASTA species
- Slow on large cohorts: increase --diann_threads and --max_cpus

## Related Tools

- quantms: DDA proteomics pipeline (https://docs.quantms.org)
- pmultiqc: QC reports (https://pmultiqc.quantms.org)
- mokume: protein quantification library (https://mokume.quantms.org)
- qpx: data format toolkit (https://qpx.quantms.org)

## Citation

Dai C et al. Nature Methods. 2024;21:1603-1607. DOI: 10.1038/s41592-024-02343-1
Demichev V et al. Nature Methods. 2020;17:41-44. DOI: 10.1038/s41592-019-0638-x
